quoteFirst: “She received a routine examination from the house staff and from
quoteLast: until seven-thirty last evening my father, Dr. Sterling, and   I went over her. We found her lungs in excellent shape, her heart   slightly enlarged, but not seriously so, her general physical   condition splendid, with the exception of the fact that she was   somewhat thin and underweight. There were no signs of any malady of   any kind whatever. Her temperature was normal, her pulse good, though   a little rapid, which, considering the circumstances was not   surprising, and her spirits commendably calm.
title: The Hospital Murders
author: Davis, Means, 1904-1999
